steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The logical relationship is contrast. Sentence 2 uses "On one hand" to introduce the first of two opposing effects of Saharan dust. The blank needs the paired transition that introduces the second, opposing effect.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — "In other words": signals a restatement. But sentence 3 describes a weakening effect opposite to sentence 2's strengthening effect — not a rephrasing of the same idea. ✗
  • Choice B — "For example": signals exemplification. The dust weakening the storm is not an example of it enhancing ice clouds; it is the opposing effect. ✗
  • Choice C — "On the other hand": is the conventional paired phrase for "On one hand," signaling the second of two opposing effects, so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — "Previously": signals a temporal shift to an earlier time. The two effects are not separated in time — they are simultaneous but opposing. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
